submit.title: Director - IT Business Solutions
submit.jd_text: Job Summary : Direct and manage IT business solutions to ensure seamless alignment with organizational digital strategy. Lead the design, delivery and optimization of business applications supporting operational efficiency, customer experience, and revenue growth. Collaborate with business and technology leaders to translate operational needs into scalable IT solutions. Key Accountabilities: Strategic Leadership: Lead the development, implementation, and governance of IT business solutions. Manage the application lifecycle including design, testing, deployment, and post-implementation support. Align business and technology strategies to drive innovation and process optimization. Oversee vendor and stakeholder relationships to ensure timely delivery and service quality. Support digital transformation and automation initiatives aligned with the company's roadmap. Ensure compliance with IT governance, cybersecurity, and data protection standards. Collaborate with cross-functional teams to evaluate business requirements and recommend suitable technology solutions. Drive continuous improvement through analytics, system performance monitoring, and end-user feedback. Define the multi-year technology roadmap for the business, aligning it with company’s broader digital and business objectives. Technology Enablement: Lead the evaluation and implementation of ERP, CRM, IoT, and data analytics solutions. Compliance & Security: Ensure all IT solutions comply with corporate governance, cybersecurity, and data protection policies. Education, Experience and Skills: Essential Qualification: Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science, Information Systems, or related field. Desirable Qualification: Master’s degree in IT or Business Management. Experience: Minimum 10–12 years in IT business solution management, with 5+ years in leadership roles managing enterprise applications or transformation projects. Skills: Strong leadership and stakeholder management skills Proven expertise in solution delivery and digital transformation Excellent communication and problem-solving abilities Strategic thinking with operational execution capability
submit.verdict_reason: IT Director, digital strategy, vendor management, transformation - textbook Ahmed fit, Dubai
submit.sonnet_reason: IT Director, digital strategy, vendor management, transformation - textbook Ahmed fit, Dubai

submit.title: Digital Consulting Director
submit.jd_text: At AlixPartners, we solve the most complex and critical challenges by moving quickly from analysis to action when it really matters; creating value that has a lasting impact on companies, their people, and the communities they serve. By understanding, respecting, and honouring the needs of our employees, clients, and communities, AlixPartners actively promotes an inclusive environment. We strongly believe in the value that diversity brings to our experiences and are committed to the perpetual enhancements of initiatives, policies, and practices. We hold ourselves accountable by providing the space for authenticity, growth, and equity for everyone. AlixPartners has embraced a hybrid work model to provide flexibility and support our employees’ work-life integration. Our hybrid model combines a mix of in-person (at client site or AlixPartners office) and remote working. Travel is part of this position, but the frequency may vary based on client, team, and individual circumstances. What You’ll Do This is an exciting time to join our growing Middle East Digital and Technology team. You will be able to help evolve our offerings in a number of our core sectors as we are supporting our clients to tackle some of their most complex business problems. You will take an integral role in identifying, shaping and cultivating new opportunities to work with clients, much of the time in close partnership with other areas of the firm. This Director opportunity is a permanent position located in Riyadh and reports to a Partner. The ideal candidate will be at home operating at the intersection of business strategy and technology, partnering with corporate C-suite executives and Private Equity Operating Partners to drive business growth, transformation, and long-term value creation. We bring a distinctive combination of deep technical expertise and sharp commercial acumen to deliver sustainable impact hands-on. Examples of our past work include helping some of the world’s best known companies leverage technology to realise their business objectives, working with private equity investors to evolve their company portfolios, and supporting high-profile turnaround cases. This description is not designed to encompass a comprehensive listing of required activities, duties or responsibilities. What You’ll Need The ideal candidate will have a mixture of 15+ years, digital consulting and industry experience Hands-on leadership experience from a highly relevant Digital and Technology role in industry, in Saudi Arabia, would be desirable Extensive blue chip consulting experience in Digital and Technology strategy development, operating model, capability and governance design is essential Experience of delivering large and complex Digital transformation and technology execution programmes with multiple workstreams Industry experience preferred in Telecom, Retail, Oil & Gas Strong experience in relationship management, proposal development and strong commercial instincts are key. In addition, an ability to generate growing demand for our wider firm capabilities and resources in prospective and existing clients Demonstrable ability to understand AI concepts and how they are applied to the technology solutions Deep expertise with Technology stack(s), IT Architecture, Technology Operating Model and Organizational Design as well as ERP solutions etc. Experience working with clients formulating hypotheses of potential business issues and reasons for financial performance, and an ability to develop data-driven and analytical approaches for improving performance using Technology You will hold valid residency for Saudi Arabia, or be able to obtain such, (administrative support will be provided by AlixPartners to obtain permits); ability to obtain work permit to travel and work across EU Excellent written and verbal communication skills in English, Arabic is advantageous Willingness to work outside of normal business hours, and in particular as unique projects/needs arise. Ability to work full time in an office and remote environment; physically able to sit/stand at a computer and work in front of a computer screen for significant portions of the workday Must become familiar with, and promote and abide by, our Core Values as defined by the AlixPartners’ Code of Conduct and foster an inclusive environment with people at all levels of an organisation Office Location: KAFD, Riyadh Working hours: 8:30am – 5:30pm, Sunday to Thursday, hybrid model The firm offers market-leading benefits that provide flexible options to support our employee’s needs, you can read more about the AlixPartners benefits here. This advert will be removed once a suitable candidate has been identified. AlixPartners is an equal opportunity employer. # LI-Hybrid
submit.verdict_reason: Strong DT director role, Riyadh, consulting firm but strategic delivery focus suits Ahmed's profile
sonnet_flags: Consulting firm - 'no consulting background' objection possible
submit.sonnet_reason: Strong DT director role, Riyadh, consulting firm but strategic delivery focus suits Ahmed's profile

submit.title: Vice President, Operations APAC-IMEA
submit.jd_text: Role Overview The VP – Operations, APAC & IMEA plays a critical leadership role in shaping the operational strategy and driving operational excellence for APAC & IMEA - Rest of World (RoW) markets. This role is accountable for business growth, operational performance (including efficiency gains, waste elimination, best practice implementation, and cost of goods management), customer satisfaction, and team development. The position combines strategic leadership with hands-on operational management to ensure Brink’s remains a benchmark for excellence across diverse markets. Responsibilities Own the P&L for assigned RoW markets, driving revenue growth, profitability, and cost efficiency. Develop and execute long-term operational strategies aligned with Brink’s global transformation agenda. Build strong partnerships with senior leadership, functional teams, and clients to deliver integrated solutions. Lead transformation initiatives including standardization, automation, and process improvement. Page 1 Establish and track operational scorecards, KPIs, and metrics that measure and drive excellence. Champion a culture of continuous improvement and Lean methodologies to optimize performance. Identify and pursue opportunities for service expansion, efficiency, and innovation. Act as a subject matter expert in operational models, route optimization, and service delivery. Build, mentor, and retain high-performing teams with a focus on diversity, inclusion, and development. Foster a culture of accountability, safety, and engagement across all levels of the business. Ensure operational practices deliver exceptional service quality and client satisfaction. Develop and maintain strong relationships with external stakeholders including regulators, clients, and strategic partners. Minimum Qualifications Bachelor’s degree in operations or a related field. 15+ years of progressive operations experience in areas such as customer service, logistics, go to-market, performance & efficiency, network optimization, and transformation. At least 5 years at a senior operational leadership level with proven senior leadership and transformational experience in multi-country operations. Strong financial acumen with demonstrated success in managing P&L, budgeting, and forecasting. Experience with Lean/continuous improvement methodologies. Desired Qualifications Advanced degree (MBA or similar). Experience in secure logistics, transportation, financial services, or related industries. Ability and willingness to travel extensively across RoW markets. Skills & Experience Expertise in operational transformation and process optimization. Ability to manage complex organizations and navigate ambiguity in global markets. Exceptional interpersonal and communication skills with the ability to influence at all levels. Proven ability to build, mentor, and retain high-performing teams. Exposure to unionized work environments is an advantage.
submit.verdict_reason: VP Operations, P&L, transformation agenda, multi-region - strong operational fit
sonnet_flags: Cash management company - niche but operational role fully transferable
submit.sonnet_reason: VP Operations, P&L, transformation agenda, multi-region - strong operational fit

Key Responsibilities - Define and execute the organisation’s IT strategy aligned with business goals - Oversee all aspects of IT, including infrastructure, networks, applications, security, hardware, and software - Lead cybersecurity initiatives, ensuring robust protection, compliance, and risk management - Advise the business on IT architecture, systems implementation, and digital transformation - Manage vendors, budgets, and IT roadmaps - Drive continuous improvement and introduce scalable processes and governance - Explore and implement emerging technologies, including AI-driven solutions, where appropriate Requirements - Proven experience in a senior IT leadership role - Strong generalist IT background across infrastructure, networks, applications, and security - Experience working in SMEs or scaling businesses, with the ability to introduce enterprise-level practices - Strategic thinker with a hands-on approach when needed - Demonstrated experience advising leadership teams and influencing business decisions - Solid understanding of cybersecurity frameworks and risk management - Exposure to or interest in AI technologies and their business applications For further information or a confidential discussion, please get in touch.

submit.title: Senior Director, Operations Excellence
submit.jd_text: The Brink’s Company (NYSE:BCO) is a leading global provider of cash and valuables management, digital retail solutions, and ATM managed services. Our customers include financial institutions, retailers, government agencies, mints, jewelers, and other commercial operations. Our network of operations in 51 countries serves customers in more than 100 countries. We believe in building partnerships that secure commerce and doing that requires fostering an engaged culture that values people with diverse backgrounds, ideas, and perspectives. We build a sense of belonging, so all employees feel respected, safe, and valued, and we provide equal opportunity to participate and grow. We are seeking an accomplished and dynamic BGS and IMEA & APAC Operations Excellence Leader to join our team in Dubai, UAE. In this role, you will play a critical part as the Operations team, contributing to our ongoing success and strategic goals. As part of the Operations Function, you will be responsible to lead the design and execution of strategies that deliver world-class operations management , driving 3% year-on-year productivity improvement and embedding a culture of continuous improvement. Key Responsibilities Strategic Leadership Develop and execute the Operational Excellence roadmap aligned with corporate objectives. Champion a culture of continuous improvement and operational discipline across all functions. Productivity & Efficiency Deliver minimum 3% YoY productivity improvement through process optimization and waste elimination. Implement world-class operations management practices to maximize resource utilization and reduce cost-to-serve. LEAN & Process Improvement Deploy LEAN tools (Value Stream Mapping, 5S, Standard Work, Visual Management) across operations. Facilitate Kaizen events and Gemba walks to identify inefficiencies and drive actionable improvements. Standardize processes and procedures to ensure scalability and consistency. Customer Satisfaction Improve operational processes to enhance service quality and customer experience. Collaborate with commercial and customer service teams to align operational improvements with client expectations. Change Management & Capability Building Develop training programs to build internal expertise in LEAN and continuous improvement. Act as a change agent, ensuring adoption of new processes and tools across the organization. Governance & Reporting Establish governance frameworks for Operational Excellence initiatives. Preferred Qualifications Bachelor’s degree in Engineering, Supply Chain, Business, or related field; Master’s preferred. 10+ years of experience in operational excellence, process improvement, or industrial engineering within logistics, supply chain, or manufacturing sectors. Proven track record of implementing LEAN, Kaizen, and Gemba practices at scale. Strong analytical and problem-solving skills with expertise in data-driven decision-making. Excellent leadership, communication, and stakeholder management skills. Proficiency in English. Strong collaborator. Can prioritize. Manage tight deadlines. Additional Requirements Willingness to travel domestically and internationally as needed. Strong organizational skills with the ability to manage multiple priorities and projects simultaneously. Flexibility to work across different time zones. Continuously identify areas for improvement.
submit.verdict_reason: Operations Excellence, Senior Director, IMEA & APAC, transformation - perfect alignment with Ahmed's background
submit.sonnet_reason: Operations Excellence, Senior Director, IMEA & APAC, transformation - perfect alignment with Ahmed's background

submit.jd_text: We're looking for a dynamic Principal, Digital Delivery to lead complex digital transformation initiatives that drive value at scale. This is a hands-on strategic delivery role; combining operational leadership with a strong consulting mindset to shape, plan and execute digital programmes across government, enterprise and public policy landscapes. WHY THIS ROLE IS EXCITING ❖ Lead multi-stakeholder digital transformation at organisational scale ❖ Influence digital strategy across sectors including government, finance, infrastructure and tech ❖ Partner with senior leadership and external stakeholders to shape outcomes ❖ Build frameworks, standards and delivery models that become the future way of working ❖ Work in a fast-evolving environment where your decisions create measurable impact What You Will Do ❖ Strategic Delivery Leadership Lead end-to-end delivery of high-impact digital and transformation programmes Design delivery models, governance and frameworks to ensure predictable, quality outcomes Translate strategy into actionable roadmaps, milestones and measurable outcomes ❖ Client & Stakeholder Partnership Serve as a senior advisor to internal teams and external partners Drive alignment across project teams, technology teams, and leadership Facilitate executive decision-making and strategic prioritisation ❖ Digital & Business Impact Champion digital best practices; including agile, lean and hybrid models Embed data-informed decision-making across delivery teams Identify risks early and design mitigation strategies that protect delivery integrity ❖ Team Leadership & Capability Building Mentor and inspire mid-senior level delivery leads and cross-functional teams Drive continuous improvement, knowledge sharing and capability uplift Champion a delivery culture that prioritises quality, clarity and collaboration Requirements WHAT YOU'LL BRING ❖ 10+ years' experience leading digital delivery or transformation programmes ❖ Demonstrated ability to deliver complex, multi-stakeholder digital initiatives ❖ Strong strategic judgement coupled with operational excellence ❖ Excellent communicator, able to influence at senior levels ❖ Comfortable in ambiguity, with a track record of structuring the unknown into clear plans What Success Looks Like ❖ Clear delivery models that reduce risk and increase predictability ❖ High-impact digital change delivered on time, with measurable outcomes ❖ Strong stakeholder trust & alignment across functions ❖ A culture of delivery excellence that scales with the organisation

submit.jd_text: We are partnering with a high-profile, government-affiliated office in the UAE seeking to appoint a Director of Operations to support a senior leadership figure on strategic, high-impact initiatives. review.title: Principal Engagement Manager
review.jd_text: The Opportunity The Digital Strategy Group (DSG) EMEA is Adobe’s strategic advisory team. We partner with the world’s leading brands to shape their digital experience vision and accelerate business transformation. Working alongside Adobe’s enterprise sales teams, DSG engages directly with customer executives. We help define digital strategies, shape future customer experiences, and build compelling cases for transformation powered by Adobe’s multi-cloud platform. As a Principal Consultant, you will act as a trusted advisor to C-suite leaders across Regulated industries (financial services, insurance, pharma, healthcare & public sector). You will support complex digital transformation initiatives and help build Adobe’s most strategic enterprise opportunities. This is a high-impact individual contributor role that combines industry expertise, critical thinking and consultative engagement. You will collaborate with sales leaders, solution consultants and partners to help customers unlock the full value of Adobe and deliver meaningful business outcomes! What You’ll Do Strategic Advisory & Executive Engagement – Partner with C-suite leaders across Regulated Industries . Shape transformation strategies and lead executive discussions aligned to Adobe’s capabilities. Vision & Experience Strategy – Co-create “north star” customer experiences. Define the capabilities required to deliver them. Value & Business Case Development – Develop value-based narratives and quantified business cases. Demonstrate the economic impact of Adobe solutions. Deal Strategy & Opportunity Acceleration – Work with enterprise sales teams to shape complex opportunities. Position Adobe as a strategic transformation partner. Industry Thought Leadership & Ecosystem Collaboration – Contribute to industry vision. Partner across sales, consulting, product and ecosystem teams to deliver high-impact engagements. What You'll Need Industry Expertise – Experience working with organisations in financial service, insurance, pharma, healthcare & public sector. Strong understanding of digital transformation trends in these sectors. Digital Transformation & Customer Experience – Knowledge of customer experience, digital marketing, data and technology-enabled engagement. Understand how these capabilities support new business models. Executive Engagement – Ability to influence C-suite and senior leaders. Translate complex ideas into clear strategic narratives. Strategic Problem Solving – Strong analytical and critical thinking skills. Ability to structure complex problems and develop clear recommendations. Consultative Collaboration – Experience supporting complex enterprise opportunities. Skilled in value storytelling, crafting financial justifications, and cross-functional collaboration. About Adobe Adobe empowers everyone to create through innovative platforms and tools that unleash creativity, productivity and personalized customer experiences. Adobe’s industry-leading offerings including Adobe Acrobat Studio, Adobe Express, Adobe Firefly, Creative Cloud, Adobe Experience Platform, Adobe Experience Manager, and GenStudio enable people and businesses to turn ideas into impact, powered by AI and driven by human ingenuity. Our 30,000+ employees worldwide are creating the future and raising the bar as we drive the next decade of growth. We’re on a mission to hire the very best and believe in creating a company culture where all employees are empowered to make an impact. At Adobe, we believe that great ideas can come from anywhere in the organization. The next big idea could be yours. Let’s Adobe together At Adobe, we believe in creating a company culture where all employees are empowered to make an impact. Learn more about Adobe life, including our values and culture, focus on people, purpose and community, Adobe for All, comprehensive benefits programs, the stories we tell, the customers we serve, and how you can help us advance our mission of empowering everyone to create. Adobe is proud to be an Equal Employment Opportunity employer. We do not discriminate based on gender, race or color, ethnicity or national origin, age, disability, religion, sexual orientation, gender identity or expression, veteran status, or any other protected characteristic. Learn more. Adobe aims to make our Careers website and recruiting process accessible to any and all users. If you have a disability or special need that requires accommodation to navigate our website or complete the application process, email accommodations@adobe.com or call +1 408-536-3015. AI Use Guidelines for Interviews: Our interviews are designed to reflect your own skills and thinking. The use of AI or recording tools during live interviews is not permitted unless explicitly invited by the interviewer or approved in advance as part of a reasonable accommodation. If these tools are used inappropriately or in a way that misrepresents your work, your application may not move forward in the process. At Adobe,
review.verdict_reason: Digital transformation advisory to C-suite - good skills fit, but requires Adobe CX platform expertise. Individual contributor consulting
review.sonnet_reason: Digital transformation advisory to C-suite - good skills fit, but requires Adobe CX platform expertise. Individual contributor consulting

review.title: VP - Digital Trade Solutions
review.jd_text: Job Summary : The role is a senior leadership role that focuses on driving digital transformation, developing technology-driven products, and enhance commercial value through digital channels. The role will spearhead the digital transformation of products, driving adoption of digital platforms, automating processes, and enhancing customer experience (user journey) to increase revenue and operational efficiency. Key Responsibilities Strategic Leadership: Develop and implement a digital strategy for trade solutions that aligns with organizational goals and market trends. Product Management & Commercialization: Lead the development, launch, and enhancement of digital trade channels, such as Port Community Systems (PCS), Supply Chain Finance platforms, and digital customs systems. Onboarding & Client Services: Manage the onboarding of clients and counterparties to digital platforms, driving adoption rates and ensuring a seamless user experience. Digital Transformation: Lead cross-functional initiatives to move from manual/paper-based trade processes to digital solutions (e.g., e-bills of lading). Operational Risk & Governance: Ensure all digital solutions are compliant with regulatory standards and internal risk frameworks, including KYC/AML in digital onboarding. Vendor & Stakeholder Management: Coordinate with external technology partners, consultants, and internal departments (IT, Sales, Legal, Operations) to implement solutions. Data Analysis & Performance Monitoring: Utilize dashboards and data insights to track transaction volumes, system performance, and ROI of digital platforms. Required Experience & Qualifications Education: A Bachelor's or Master’s degree in Business, Finance, Information Technology, or a related field. Experience: 7+ years of experience in trade finance, banking, or logistics technology, with proven success in leading digital product launches. Digital Literacy: Deep understanding of trade finance technologies (e.g., SWIFT, blockchain), FinTech solutions, and digital banking platforms. Leadership Skills: Proven ability to manage teams, influence stakeholders, and drive projects in a fast-paced environment.
review.verdict_reason: Digital transformation angle exists but role is trade finance product management - Port Community Systems, customs digital
review.sonnet_reason: Digital transformation angle exists but role is trade finance product management - Port Community Systems, customs digital

review.title: Head of Application Support - MSIT du
review.jd_text: About This Opportunity Accountable for end-to-end service delivery, service performance, and continuous improvement across multiple IT service lines, ensuring contractual compliance, service stability, operational efficiency, and customer satisfaction, in alignment with Ericsson Managed Services standards. What You Will Do Accountable for 24x7 service delivery across IT Landscape (OSS/BSS) in line with customer contract. Ensure consistent SLA, KPI, and compliance adherence across incident, problem, change, and release management. Provide leadership and governance to Service Delivery (L1/L2/L3) teams, including partners. Act as escalation authority for major service issues and chronic service risks. Act as Enterprise / E2E Architect for operational stability and transformation initiatives. Drive Continuous Service Improvement (CSI) initiatives to improve service quality, stability, and efficiency. Ensure effective major incident management, including executive-level communication and root cause closure. Drive standardization and adoption of tools, processes, and automation across service line. Proactively identify service risks and ensure mitigation and recovery plans are in place. Own consolidated service reporting and provide fact-based performance insights to customers and internal stakeholders. Contribute to cost governance, productivity improvements, and operational efficiency targets. Develop service delivery leadership capability and ensure succession planning and skill readiness. Hands-on operations responsibility for managing third-party applications and production services (e.g., Siebel CRM, HP Service Assurance, HP Mediation, Ericsson Billing & Charging, digital channels — App/Web/Selfcare/eShop, CVM such as Pega, etc.). Provide line management of large onsite and offshore teams (50+ employees), including resource planning, performance management and capability development. Support business development, growth and pre-sales activities related to managed services offerings. Ensure 24/7 operational readiness and decision-making (on-call leadership and ability to deliver under pressure). Promote and lead adoption of operations automation and TM Forum L4 capabilities, including AIOps and other automation tools. You will bring: Bachelor’s degree in engineering, IT, or equivalent. 15–18+ years of experience in IT service delivery and operations, with a minimum of 15 years in Managed Services IT (MSIT) roles. Proven experience leading large-scale managed services delivery in telecom or complex IT environments (including knowledge on Ericsson products like BSCS, Charging, Mediation or similar COTS products from other vendors). Hands-on operations experience managing third-party applications and digital channels (Siebel CRM, HP SA, HP Mediation, Ericsson Billing & Charging, App/Web/Selfcare/eShop, CVM/Pega, etc.). Line management track record managing large teams onsite and offshore (50+ employees). Strong expertise in ITIL-based service management and CSI. Hands-on experience or strong working knowledge of automation aligned to TM Forum Autonomous Operations (L4), including exposure to AIOps platforms and their application in production IT operations; deep knowledge of operations automation tools is a plus. Extensive experience in customer-facing senior delivery roles. End-to-end service ownership. Senior stakeholder and customer management. Risk management and decision making. Data-driven governance and reporting. Leadership development and mentoring; people coaching skills. Continuous improvement and efficiency mindset. Leadership and soft skills including: customer interfacing, ability to deliver under pressure, a 24/7 mindset, business development / pre-sales orientation, strong presentation and communication skills. Certifications (Preferred) ITIL eTOM PMP (Preferred) Scrum Master Why join Ericsson? At Ericsson, you´ll have an outstanding opportunity. The chance to use your skills and imagination to push the boundaries of what´s possible. To build solutions never seen before to some of the world’s toughest problems. You´ll be challenged, but you won’t be alone. You´ll be joining a team of diverse innovators, all driven to go beyond the status quo to craft what comes next. What happens once you apply? Click Here to find all you need to know about what our typical hiring process looks like. Encouraging a diverse and inclusive organization is core to our values at Ericsson, that's why we champion it in everything we do. We truly believe that by collaborating with people with different experiences we drive innovation, which is essential for our future growth. We encourage people from all backgrounds to apply and realize their full potential as part of our Ericsson team. Ericsson is proud to be an Equal Opportunity Employer. learn more. Primary country and city: United Arab Emirates (AE) || Dubai Req ID: 779653
review.verdict_reason: Service delivery, ITIL, telecom (du), governance, SLA management - skills overlap with Ahmed's operational background
review.sonnet_reason: Service delivery, ITIL, telecom (du), governance, SLA management - skills overlap with Ahmed's operational background

review.title: Head of Operations
review.location: Abu Dhabi Emirate, United Arab Emirates
review.jd_text: Head of Operations We are currently partnering with a confidential organisation operating within the financial markets and trading infrastructure space, who are looking to appoint a senior operations leader to help drive the next phase of growth. This is a high impact leadership opportunity for someone who can bring structure, resilience, and commercial thinking into a fast moving, highly regulated environment. The successful individual will take ownership of the day to day operational backbone of the business, ensuring smooth platform operations, strong governance, effective risk controls, and close alignment across technology, compliance, and wider business functions. The role will also play a key part in strengthening operational scalability, business continuity, and external stakeholder integration as the organisation continues to evolve. This opportunity would suit someone who enjoys building operational maturity in complex environments and who can operate as a strategic partner to leadership while still remaining close enough to execution. It offers strong visibility, broad scope, and the chance to influence how a growing platform is shaped from an infrastructure and operational perspective. Key requirements: • Proven senior leadership experience within an exchange, trading platform, capital markets, or similarly regulated environment • Strong understanding of operational infrastructure, risk, governance, and business continuity • Ability to work cross functionally with technology, product, and compliance teams • Exposure to scaling operations in a fast paced or evolving business • Commercially minded, with the gravitas to engage both internally and externally
review.verdict_reason: Financial markets/trading infrastructure ops - specialized domain. Operations leadership transferable but niche sector

review.title: VP, Business Planning
review.location: Abu Dhabi, Abu Dhabi Emirate, United Arab Emirates
review.jd_text: Join the UAE’s largest bank and one of the world’s largest and safest financial institutions. Our focus is to create value for our employees, customers, shareholders and communities to grow through differentiation, agility and innovation. We are looking for top talent and your success is our success. Accelerate your growth as you help us reach our goals and advance your career. Be ready to make your mark a top company, in an exciting & dynamic industry. Job Purpose The role is responsible for partnering with WBG, Enablement & Control partners at a group level to drive business management activities such as Strategy and Business Development, Business & Performance Management, Data and Analytics and Lighthouse enhancements Key Accountabilities Key Tasks & Activities: Collaborate with business heads to drive Strategy and Business Development initiatives and assess impact on operational readiness and customer experience. Play a key role in setting up and tracking balanced scorecards for coverage and product teams, lead business management initiatives by collaborating with various internal stakeholders such as Credit, Compliance, Operations, IT, CX, Risk etc. Leverage data analytics to identify potential opportunity to improve overall business performance (revenue and/or cost). Lead simplification of CX E2E journeys to create transformational experiences through digital assets. Drive WBG-wide communication for relevant initiatives (as required). Develop effective relationships including spearheading Change Management within WBG. Behavioural Skills Self-starter - the incumbent must be able to lead workshops and help formulate requirements, options and solutions with an ability to get things done through others; able to identify proactive, innovative solutions to meet the changing needs of the business. Strong analytical and problem-solving mindset. Customer Focus - understand customer perspective when developing requirements and ensuring fit for purpose customer friendly solution are designed. Strong communication skills are required to draw out requirements and to challenge where appropriate and to establish bond with key stakeholders. A high degree of self-assurance/initiative and business sense is required to perform responsibilities independently or collaboratively in a team environment as required Qualifications & Experience Minimum Qualification: Experience of banking industry with deep understanding on the Wholesale Banking. Must have master's or bachelor's degree in information technology or relevant discipline. Minimum Experience 12+ years of experience in Financial Services, Strategy function, Business management and/or Technology execution. With strong written and verbal communication skills with a talent for precise articulations of customer problems. Must have 8+ years of experience in the banking domain. Hands-on experience in executing transformation objectives with ability to structure, manage complex business challenges to advance business and digital strategies. Strong User engagement and communication skills / Ability to contribute to and implement organizational strategy. Capacity to work with people at all levels in an organization and with people from many cultural backgrounds. Able to manage effectively a number of competing priorities. Strategic thinker with ability to lead by example demonstrating an ability to deliver set tasks, objectives and strategic priorities. Project Management Professional. Proficient in MS Office with advanced Excel skills Data Science & Business Analytics, Financial Analysis & Reports.

review.title: Executive Director, API & Open Banking -Cash and Trade
review.location: Abu Dhabi, Abu Dhabi Emirate, United Arab Emirates
review.jd_text: Join the UAE’s largest bank and one of the world’s largest and safest financial institutions. Our focus is to create value for our employees, customers, shareholders and communities to grow through differentiation, agility and innovation. We are looking for top talent and your success is our success. Accelerate your growth as you help us reach our goals and advance your career. Be ready to make your mark a top company, in an exciting & dynamic industry. Job Purpose The individual is responsible for defining, owning and scaling the bank’s API‑led propositions across Global Trade, Treasury Services and Tokenized / Digital Asset‑enabled offerings. The role owns the strategic product vision, commercialization, monetization and enterprise‑wide adoption of API‑based capabilities, positioning APIs and Open Banking as a core distribution and engagement layer that embeds the bank into client ecosystems, enables real‑time financial flows and supports emerging use cases across the current and future state of transaction banking. The role acts as a strategic bridge between business, technology, digital transformation and regulatory bodies, ensuring that all API‑led products are client‑centric, scalable, secure and fully compliant with UAE and international regulatory frameworks. Key Accountabilities Contribute to the development and execution of GTx and Wholesale Banking API and Open banking strategy across domestic and international locations. Define and own the API product vision and long‑term roadmap aligned with Global Transaction Banking, Treasury and Tokenization strategy. Position API and Open Banking capabilities as core differentiator for payments, liquidity management, escrow services and Next Gen propositions Identify collaborate-to- create opportunities with Fintech partners for innovative use cases for Cash and Trade propositions. Drive harmonization and consistency across product lines to create a coherent, scalable API and Open Banking stack for all cash and Trade Product verticals. Drive collaboration across business, technology and control functions. Ensure alignment with FAB policies, frameworks and operating standards. Represent the function effectively to senior management, external and internal stakeholders Job Context Product Vision & Strategic Ownership Define and own the API product vision and multi‑year roadmap aligned to Global Trade, Treasury and Tokenization strategy. Position APIs as core channels for payments, liquidity, escrow, trade, and digital asset‑enabled offerings. Identify and prioritize embedded finance, ecosystem‑led growth and tokenization‑driven opportunities through API and Open Banking infrastructure Drive consistency and scalability across product lines to deliver a cohesive enterprise API proposition. End‑to‑End Product Lifecycle Management Own the full API product lifecycle from ideation and design through launch, scale and ongoing optimization. Ensure API products are commercially viable, reusable, secure and client‑centric. Balance speed‑to‑market with long‑term resilience, operational robustness and regulatory expectations. Client, Platform & Ecosystem Enablement Enable and scale direct integration of bank capabilities into corporate ERP, TMS, digital platforms and partner ecosystems. Engage strategic clients and partners to co‑create and scale API‑led use cases Act as senior product representative in client, partnership and industry engagements. Commercialization & Value Realization Define pricing, packaging and usage‑based commercial models for API products. Track and demonstrate value across CASA growth, fee income, client acquisition and retention. Leverage adoption analytics and performance insights to refine investment priorities and product strategy. Governance, Risk & Regulatory Alignment Ensure API products are compliant with client money, safeguarding, operational resilience and data requirements. Maintain alignment with Central Bank, FSRA / ADGM and other applicable regulatory frameworks for developing fit-for-purpose propositions in these jurisdictions Embed risk controls, transparency and reporting by design within all API solutions. Leadership & Stakeholder Management Act as a trusted senior partner to GTB, Treasury, Digital Assets, Technology, Risk and Operations. Provide clear, commercially grounded updates to the Managing Director and senior management forums. General Responsibilities Promote API reuse and standardization to reduce duplication and accelerate delivery. Lead cross-functional groups to align API strategy with enterprise goals. Maintain high standards of API documentation. Contribute to budget planning and resource allocation for API engineering. Provide training and enablement on API best practices. Qualifications & Experience 10+ years of experience in transaction banking, treasury, digital platforms or product‑led financial services. Proven track record of building and scaling API‑driven or platform‑based products in regulated environmen

review.title: Head of Enterprise Data Platforms
review.location: Abu Dhabi, Abu Dhabi Emirate, United Arab Emirates
review.jd_text: Job Description To lead the strategic design, development, simplification , modernization and governance of the bank’s enterprise data platforms, ensuring it serves as a robust, scalable, and secure foundation for data-driven decision-making, advanced analytics, AI & agentic initiatives. The role is pivotal in enabling the bank’s digital transformation by delivering a modern data architecture that supports real-time insights, multi disciplinary autonomous AI agents, regulatory compliance, and innovation across all business units. Key Functions Define and execute the enterprise data platform strategy in alignment with the bank’s digital, agentic and AI roadmap. Lead the design and implementation of a modern, cloud-based data architecture (e.g., data lakehouse, data fabric). Strategize, lead and execute the data estate simplification agenda and seamlessly decommission the redundant, obsolete and non scalable data platforms and duplicate tooling Oversee the integration of structured and unstructured data sources across the enterprise. Ensure high availability, scalability, and performance of the data platform. Establish and enforce data governance, metadata management, and data lineage practices along side Data Governance unit. Collaborate with data science, analytics, and business / OPS teams to enable self-service data access and advanced analytics. Drive the adoption of DevOps and DataOps practices for continuous delivery and automation. Manage vendor relationships and evaluate emerging technologies for platform enhancement. Ensure compliance with regulatory requirements and internal audit standards Lead, mentor, and develop a high-performing team of data engineers, architects, and platform specialists. Develop and manage the data platform budget, ensuring cost-effective operations. Report on platform performance, adoption/ usage metrics, and value delivery to executive leadership. Foster a culture of innovation, agility, and continuous improvement within the data function. Represent the bank in industry forums and contribute to thought leadership in data platform strategy. Qualifications Strategic Thinking & Vision Ability to align data platform strategy with business goals, platform-based architecture, data estate modernization and digital transformation initiatives for AI enablement. Technical Expertise Deep knowledge of cloud-native data platforms (e.g., Azure, AWS, GCP), data lake houses (Data Bricks), data mesh, and real-time data streaming (Confluent Kafka). Leadership & Influence Proven experience in leading cross-functional teams and influencing senior stakeholders. Data Governance & Security Strong understanding of data privacy regulations (e.g., UAE Central Bank, GDPR), data quality frameworks, and security best practices. Program & Change Management Expertise in managing large-scale data platform programs and driving organizational change. Collaboration & Communication Excellent interpersonal and communication skills to work across business, IT, and regulatory functions. TCO & Operational Efficiency Mindset Proven track record in optimizing TCO for data estate with FINOPS implementation and tooling simplification strategies and execution
review.verdict_reason: Data platform architecture role requires hands-on data engineering (data lakehouse, data fabric) - not Ahmed's core
review.sonnet_reason: Data platform architecture role requires hands-on data engineering (data lakehouse, data fabric) - not Ahmed's core

review.title: Principal Solution Implementation Manager
review.location: Abu Dhabi, Abu Dhabi Emirate, United Arab Emirates
review.jd_text: At Anaplan, we are a team of innovators focused on optimizing business decision-making through our leading AI-infused scenario planning and analysis platform so our customers can outpace their competition and the market. What unites Anaplanners across teams and geographies is our collective commitment to our customers’ success and to our Winning Culture. Our customers rank among the who’s who in the Fortune 50. Coca-Cola, LinkedIn, Adobe, LVMH and Bayer are just a few of the 2,400+ global companies who rely on our best-in-class platform. Our Winning Culture is the engine that drives our teams of innovators. We champion diversity of thought and ideas, we behave like leaders regardless of title, we are committed to achieving ambitious goals, and we love celebrating our wins – big and small. Supported by operating principles of being strategy-led, values-based and disciplined in execution, you’ll be inspired, connected, developed and rewarded here. Everything that makes you unique is welcome; join us and let’s build what’s next - together! Strategic Consulting Meets Expert Delivery: Lead Anaplan's Enterprise Implementations Are you a seasoned consultant with a passion for seeing strategy through to flawless execution? We are looking for a Principal Solution Implementation Manager to lead Anaplan’s most complex and strategic customer engagements across the Middle East. This role is a unique hybrid of high-end consulting and hands-on program leadership. You will be the senior-most delivery advisor for our most important enterprise accounts, acting as the bridge between the customer’s strategic vision and the technical reality of implementation. You won’t just manage projects; you’ll serve as a trusted partner to C-level stakeholders, guiding them through large-scale business transformation. Based in our Abu Dhabi office with hybrid flexibility, you will be instrumental in shaping the success of iconic brands by delivering sophisticated, high-impact planning solutions. Your Impact Act as the lead strategic advisor for a portfolio of enterprise-level Anaplan deployments, translating complex business challenges into scalable solutions. Provide expert counsel to customer executives on governance, change management, project scoping, and long-term value realisation. Build and maintain C-suite relationships, operating as a trusted, non-sales partner focused entirely on the customer’s success. Orchestrate the end-to-end program, steering internal teams, partner consultants, and customer stakeholders toward delivery excellence. Command complex programs, mastering scope, risk, and budget to ensure predictable, high-quality outcomes. Intervene and recover at-risk projects, applying your advisory and leadership skills to navigate challenges and bring deployments back on track. Your Qualifications A background in management consulting, technology consulting, or a client-facing professional services role is essential. Multiple years of experience leading large-scale enterprise program delivery and execution, ideally from within a consultancy or a SaaS professional services organisation. ERP or EPM expertise in at least one of the following: Supply Chain, Financial Planning & Analysis (FP&A), or Workforce Planning. Demonstrated ability to operate as a strategic advisor, with outstanding influencing and negotiation skills at the executive level. A track record of successfully delivering complex, multi-year initiatives against tight timelines and budgets. A resilient, adaptable, and proactive mindset, with a passion for solving complex business problems. Fluent communication skills in English and Arabic Our Commitment to Diversity, Equity, Inclusion and Belonging (DEIB) We believe attracting and retaining the best talent and fostering an inclusive culture strengthens our business. DEIB improves our workforce, enhances trust with our partners and customers, and drives business success. Build your career in a place where diversity, equity, inclusion and belonging aren’t just words on paper – this is what drives our innovation, it’s how we connect, and it contributes to what makes us a market leader. We believe in a hiring and working environment where all people are respected and valued, regardless of gender identity or expression, sexual orientation, religion, ethnicity, age, neurodiversity, disability status, citizenship, or any other aspect which makes people unique. We hire you for who you are, and we want you to bring your authentic self to work every day! We will ensure that individuals with disabilities are provided reasonable accommodation to participate in the job application or interview process, perform essential job functions, and receive equitable benefits and all privileges of employment. Please contact us to request accommodation. Fraud Recruitment Disclaimer It has come to our attention that fraudulent and fictitious job opportunities are being circulated on the Internet. Prospective candidates are being contacted
review.verdict_reason: Implementation/delivery management has PMO overlap. Anaplan EPM platform - connected planning. Abu Dhabi
review.sonnet_reason: Implementation/delivery management has PMO overlap. Anaplan EPM platform - connected planning. Abu Dhabi

review.title: IT & Systems Director
review.jd_text: IT & Systems Director - on behalf of a client LOCATION : DIFC, Dubai SALARY : Depending on experience ABOUT US We are launching a brand-new and exclusive members' club, located in the heart of Dubai, that offers a private world of unparalleled connections. Representing a new pinnacle of sophistication and elegance in the region, the club sets itself apart as a carefully curated membership offering, fusing Emirati cultural nuance with timeless heritage hospitality. We are committed to providing exceptional service and creating unforgettable experiences for our distinguished members. ROLE OVERVEW The IT & Systems Director holds executive responsibility for the club’s technology ecosystem, ensuring all digital platforms, infrastructure and operational systems function as a secure, resilient and fully integrated environment. This includes CRM, POS, finance systems, member-facing platforms, access control and data governance. Reporting to the Club General Manager, the role ensures technology supports the full member lifecycle while enabling efficient operations and high service standards across the club. The IT & Systems Director oversees systems procurement, integration and vendor management, ensuring platforms operate as a coherent and scalable architecture aligned with the club’s membership strategy, privacy standards and long-term growth. KEY RESPONSIBILITIES System Strategy & Architecture Own the end-to-end systems architecture, with Salesforce as the core CRM and system of record . Define how all digital and operational systems connect, interact and evolve over time. Ensure technology supports the full member lifecycle, from first introduction to long-term retention. Establish clear principles for privacy, data governance, security and system ownership across the stack. Ensure infrastructure resilience, cybersecurity standards and operational continuity across all systems. Salesforce & Core Platform Ownership Lead the i n-house implementation, configuration and ongoing ownership of Salesforce . Design Salesforce architecture to support: Prospecting, referrals and provenance tracking, Applications, committee workflows and approvals, KYC, onboarding and founder engagement, Member engagement, usage and retention. Act as internal product owner, translating operational and commercial needs into Salesforce delivery. Configure objects, workflows, automations, dashboards and reporting aligned to the approved membership operating model. Systems Integration & Delivery Oversee integration of Salesforce with all core systems, including: Website and application flows, Member app and portal, POS, reservations and table management, Access control and identity systems, Payments and finance. Ensure integrations are stable, well-documented and future-proofed. Minimise dependency on external vendors by retaining system knowledge in-house. Selectively manage specialist external partners where required, with clear accountability and quality control. Ensure alignment between CRM, POS, finance and operational systems to maintain a unified data environment. Manage technology vendors and implementation partners, ensuring performance, accountability and knowledge retention in-house. Pre-opening Implementation Lead delivery of a fully integrated systems environment ready for soft opening. Ensure all systems are tested end-to-end against real operational scenarios. Align systems delivery tightly with the pre-opening critical path. Enable founder onboarding, early bookings and pre-opening engagement through digital channels. Live Operations & Optimisation Own systems performance once the club is live, ensuring reliability, data quality and operational clarity. Continually optimise systems based on member behaviour, staff feedback and operational learnings. Support Membership, Brand, Operations and Finance teams with insight, reporting and system-driven decision-making. Introduce phased enhancements to automation, personalisation and intelligence over time. Build internal documentation, training and handover processes to ensure long-term resilience. SALESFORCE EXPERIENCE Please note, this role requires deep Salesforce experience and applications without this will not be considered. While formal certification is not a substitute for real-world delivery, the successful candidate is expected to demonstrate strong Salesforce platform expertise, supported by relevant credentials or equivalent hands-on experience. Candidates should have personally led Salesforce delivery within complex, live operational environments. REQUIREMENTS Must be currently based in Dubai or available to relocate to Dubai for a full-time role. 10+ experience in senior IT, systems or digital leadership roles within hospitality, private clubs, luxury environments or comparable service-led organisations. Deep, hands-on experience owning Salesforce implementations in-house, from architecture and configuration through to live operation and optimisation. Demonstrated abilit
review.verdict_reason: Private members club DIFC - IT Director level right but company is small-scale, below Ahmed's enterprise impact level

review.title: Enterprise Programs Director
review.jd_text:
The driving force behind our success has always been the people of AspenTech. What drives us, is our aspiration, our desire and ambition to keep pushing the envelope, overcoming any hurdle, challenging the status quo to continually find a better way. You will experience these qualities of passion, pride and aspiration in many ways — from a rich set of career development programs to support of community service projects to social events that foster fun and relationship building across our global community.

The Role

The Enterprise Programs Director will find, land, and execute global enterprise deals. The ideal candidate thrives at the intersection of business transformation and technology enablement—able to engage confidently with C-level executives, guide large delivery teams, and drive high-impact outcomes across global enterprises. This role requires someone who can translate business objectives into structured, executable programs while fostering strong client relationships and leading with influence across matrixed environments.

Your Impact

Strategic Leadership: Direct complex customer engagements, both during the assessment (pre-deal) and implementation phase, providing continuity for our customers and ensuring alignment with corporate objectives and client needs.Stakeholder Engagement: Build and maintain strong relationships with C-level executives and key stakeholders across multiple customer levels.Team Management: Lead large teams of consultants, partners and professional services colleagues, fostering a collaborative and high-performing environment.Program Execution: Ensure the successful delivery of enterprise assessments and deployment programs, managing scope, budget, timelines, and resources effectively. Continuously identify opportunities to enhance program delivery and drive operational excellence.Operating in an advisory capacity to identify key problems, develop and evaluate alternative solutions and make recommendations to key customers and stakeholders.

What You'll Need

The successful candidate will have a minimum of twelve (12) years’ experience with Information Technology initiatives, including formal project leadership experience.At least 4 years of Management Consulting experience at a large firm, with the corresponding skillset: business acumen, structured communication style, ability to synthesize complex information, concisely communicate with executive level presentation skillsExperience with data integration and management platformsOutstanding problem solving and analytical skills to align with the customer on the desired outcomes and be able to convert a solution vision into a tangible execution planStrong analytical skills and ability to translate business requirements into a set of project deliverables and associated tasks.Experience in managing large scale projects impacting multiple business functions at multiple locations, including international.Strong communications skills (written and oral), with an ability to listen and to articulate project status and issues. Communication experience should include presentations at all organizational levels.Team building and management skills, to bring a diverse group together to deliver the required business results through influence and execution.Technical understanding to enable effective delivery of technology projects including new systems implementations and upgrades to existing systems.Good working understanding of the project management lifecycle and the key knowledge areas of project management: integration, scope, time, cost, quality, human resource, communications, risk, procurement, stakeholder.Customer focus - proven track record of customer satisfaction and delivery success and ability to establish and maintain appropriate relationships with business and IT stakeholders.Industry recognized Project Management certification desirable.Familiarity with AspenTech's tools and solution is a plusFluent in English, Secondary language preferred in supporting region
review.verdict_reason: Aspen is industrial software (oil/gas/energy sector). Role is enterprise sales-oriented - 'find, land, execute deals'
review.sonnet_reason: Aspen is industrial software (oil/gas/energy sector). Role is enterprise sales-oriented - 'find, land, execute deals'

review.title: Chief Technology Officer
review.jd_text: Company Description City School Holdings International (CSHI) is a global education organization managing schools across six countries, including the UAE, Saudi Arabia, Pakistan, Oman, Malaysia, and the Philippines. Established in 1978, CSHI has over 45 years of expertise in education management. The organization serves more than 150,000 students worldwide, offering academic programs from Foundation Stage (FS) to A Levels. CSHI is dedicated to delivering high-quality education and shaping the future of young learners across diverse communities. Role Overview The CTO will drive enterprise-wide technology governance, infrastructure management, and in-house software development. This is a strategic and hands-on leadership role for a technology leader capable of building systems from the ground up while supporting multi-campus and international expansion. Key Responsibilities Technology Strategy & Governance Develop and execute a 3–5 years technology roadmap aligned with growth strategy. Lead digital transformation across academic, finance, HR, and operational functions. Establish IT governance, compliance, and risk management frameworks. Report technology strategy and performance metrics to the Board. ERP & Enterprise Systems (SAP Mandatory) Lead ERP implementation, integration, and optimization. Ensure seamless integration across Finance, HRMS, Admissions, and Academic systems. Drive automation, data accuracy, and reporting standardization. Manage ERP vendors and system performance. In-House Software Development (Critical) Architect and develop a proprietary School Management System (SMS). Build and lead an internal software engineering team. Oversee development of student information, attendance, grading, parent portal, teacher dashboard, and fee modules. Ensure cloud scalability (AWS/Azure), API integrations, and strong security architecture. Implement Agile and DevOps practices. Infrastructure, Cybersecurity & Innovation Ensure secure, high-availability IT infrastructure across campuses. Implement cybersecurity, disaster recovery, and compliance frameworks. Integrate EdTech tools, analytics dashboards, AI, and automation initiatives. Qualifications & Experience Master’s degree in computer science, IT, Software Engineering, or related field. 12–15+ years of progressive IT leadership experience. Minimum 5+ years in a senior technology leadership role. Proven experience in: SAP ERP implementation Large-scale system integration Leading in-house software development teams Education sector experience preferred but not mandatory. Ideal Profile Experience managing multi-site organizations. Strong Board-level communication and stakeholder management skills. Proven ability to build scalable digital platforms from the ground up.
review.verdict_reason: Education sector not Ahmed domain; JD explicitly says 'strategic AND hands-on' with in-house software dev expectation
review.sonnet_reason: Education sector not Ahmed domain; JD explicitly says 'strategic AND hands-on' with in-house software dev expectation
